description: A vertical candid arrest/custody photograph with no overlaid graphic text, slogans, or agency seals. It shows a detained man seated in the back seat of a vehicle (dark gray cloth/leather upholstery and headrests visible), apparently photographed by an officer after arrest. The man is a bearded adult with short dark hair and a mustache/goatee, wearing a gray Patagonia puffer/down jacket over a white t-shirt; his hands appear restrained at his lap and a seatbelt-style restraint or cuff is visible at the bottom of the frame. Through the rear window behind him, an industrial/parking-structure setting with cracked or frosted glass is faintly visible. He looks directly at the camera with a neutral expression. This is a documentary in-custody photograph used by ICE to publicize the arrest of a named fugitive (per the tweet, a Brazilian national with a final order of removal and DUI/negligent-driving convictions), not a designed graphic. No on-image text. Target audience: general public, demonstrating ICE apprehension of a criminal fugitive.
notable_text: (no overlaid graphic text; only 'Patagonia' jacket branding)
